name: Twins IQ Scores
description: IQ scores of identical twins raised apart: foster home vs biological parents. n=27 pairs.
studyDescription: IQ scores of 27 pairs of identical (monozygotic) twins who were separated at birth and raised in different environments — one twin raised by biological parents and the other in a foster home. This is an observational study of the effects of rearing environment on intelligence.

variableDescriptions.foster: IQ score of the twin raised in a foster home
variableDescriptions.biological: IQ score of the twin raised by biological parents

sourceDetail: OpenIntro IMS Ch. 21, openintro::twins. Original data from Burt, C. (1966). 'The genetic determination of differences in intelligence: A study of monozygotic twins reared together and apart.' British Journal of Psychology, 57(1-2), 137-153.

context.nullClaim: no difference in IQ between foster and biological twins

inferenceContexts.parameter: the population mean difference in IQ (foster − biological)
inferenceContexts.claim: there is a difference in average IQ between twins raised in foster homes and those raised by biological parents
